refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle to meet or exceed the customer-defined target under specified duty cycle and site conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; refining it to focus on minimizing cycle time clarifies the performance goal and aligns with the acceptance criteria. This enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time p95 under representative duty cycles and site conditions, comparing against CT_target.
sub_frs: FR4: Coordinate operations to minimize idle/wait time

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced hydraulic and mechanical system design for faster load, travel, and dump operations
DP2: Intelligent cycle scheduling and coordination algorithms
DP3: Real-time cycle time monitoring and adaptive control
DP4: Operator assistance and automation features
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Intelligent cycle scheduling and coordination algorithms

potential_sources: Interaction between scheduling algorithms and mechanical system performance
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically decouples cycle coordination from physical actuation, enabling modular design and clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes mechanical systems can meet timing demands
Risk that scheduling algorithms may not adapt well to unexpected site conditions
High safety criticality requires robust fail-safe coordination
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring (DP3) in next decomposition level to mitigate adaptation risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level coordination and scheduling of load→travel→dump cycle

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time vehicle and site data to inform scheduling decisions

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends operation commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Coordinates with Safety Services to ensure safe operation during cycle

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as scheduling logic is centralized here; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module mainly executes commands; contains some local control but not scheduling.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not cycle scheduling.
Safety Services provide alerts and monitoring, not scheduling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing cycle scheduling in NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and enables clear ownership and interface management.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too complex
Interface latency could impact real-time scheduling effectiveness
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor complexity growth in NGHE Solution; consider decomposing scheduling logic if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (intelligent scheduling) directly addresses refined FR sub-functions and supports cycle time optimization.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized scheduling logic in NGHE Solution module
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support clear module ownership and manageable interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and cycle time measurements is feasible at NGHE Solution module level.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of scheduling DP to NGHE Solution IM is clear and aligns with stakeholder responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Plan next level decomposition to address real-time monitoring and operator assistance.
Evaluate interface latency impact on scheduling performance.

decision.justification: While the decomposition is sound, the high safety criticality and risk of scheduling adaptation warrant adding real-time monitoring DP explicitly at this level to reduce risk and improve robustness.
next_level_sub_frs: FR4: Coordinate operations to minimize idle/wait time

revise.why: To explicitly include real-time monitoring and adaptive control as a DP to mitigate risks related to site condition variability and safety.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely
open_risks: Scheduling algorithms may not adapt well to unexpected conditions
evidence_needed: Performance data on scheduling algorithm under variable site conditions
Latency measurements of interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules
